# @fluenti/core

> Framework-agnostic compile-time i18n core for JavaScript/TypeScript. Provides ICU MessageFormat parsing, compilation, interpolation, CLDR plural rules, date/number/relative-time formatters via Intl APIs, locale negotiation, RTL detection, and SSR utilities. Used as the foundation for @fluenti/vue, @fluenti/react, and @fluenti/solid.

> **Note:** Application code should use framework packages (@fluenti/vue, @fluenti/react, @fluenti/solid)
> with their compile-time t`` tagged templates, not core's runtime APIs directly.
> Core provides the low-level engine — framework packages provide the recommended authoring surface.

- Package: `@fluenti/core`
- Docs: https://fluenti.dev
- Repository: https://github.com/usefluenti/fluenti/tree/main/packages/core
- License: MIT

## Installation

```bash
pnpm add @fluenti/core
```

Most users do not install `@fluenti/core` directly -- it is included as a dependency of `@fluenti/vue` and `@fluenti/solid`.

## createFluent(config)

Create a fully configured i18n instance. This is the main entry point for most applications.

```ts
import { createFluent } from '@fluenti/core'

const i18n = createFluent({
  locale: 'en',
  fallbackLocale: 'en',
  messages: {
    en: { greeting: 'Hello {name}!' },
    ja: { greeting: 'こんにちは {name}！' },
  },
  dateFormats: { short: { dateStyle: 'short' } },
  numberFormats: { currency: { style: 'currency', currency: 'USD' } },
  fallbackChain: { 'zh-TW': ['zh-CN', 'en'], '*': ['en'] },
  missing: (locale, id) => `[${locale}:${id}]`,
})

i18n.t('greeting', { name: 'World' }) // "Hello World!"
i18n.setLocale('ja')
i18n.t('greeting', { name: 'World' }) // "こんにちは World！"
```

### Config: FluentConfigExtended

| Property | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `locale` | `Locale` | Yes | Current active locale |
| `messages` | `AllMessages` | Yes | `Record<Locale, Messages>` -- all locale messages |
| `fallbackLocale` | `Locale` | No | Single fallback locale when key not found in current locale |
| `fallbackChain` | `Record<string, Locale[]>` | No | Per-locale or wildcard (`'*'`) fallback chains |
| `missing` | `(locale, id) => string \| undefined` | No | Handler for missing translations; return undefined to fall through to ID |
| `dateFormats` | `DateFormatOptions` | No | Custom named date format styles, merged over defaults |
| `numberFormats` | `NumberFormatOptions` | No | Custom named number format styles, merged over defaults |
| `namespaceMapping` | `NamespaceMapping` | No | Glob-pattern to namespace mapping |
| `externalCatalogs` | `Array<{ package, catalogDir }>` | No | External catalog sources |

### Instance: FluentInstanceExtended

| Method | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `t(id, values?)` | `(string \| MessageDescriptor, Record<string, unknown>?) => string` | Translate a message by ID or descriptor |
| `d(value, style?)` | `(Date \| number, string?) => string` | Format a date using a named style |
| `n(value, style?)` | `(number, string?) => string` | Format a number using a named style |
| `format(message, values?)` | `(string, Record<string, unknown>?) => string` | Interpolate an ICU message string directly (no catalog lookup) |
| `setLocale(locale)` | `(Locale) => void` | Switch the active locale |
| `loadMessages(locale, messages)` | `(Locale, Messages) => void` | Load or merge messages for a locale at runtime |
| `getLocales()` | `() => Locale[]` | List all locales with loaded messages |
| `locale` | `Locale` (getter/setter) | Get or set the current locale |

The deprecated `tRaw()` method is an alias for `format()` and will be removed in a future major version.

### Message Resolution Order

1. Current locale catalog
2. `fallbackLocale` (if configured)
3. `fallbackChain[currentLocale]` (locale-specific chain)
4. `fallbackChain['*']` (wildcard chain)
5. `missing(locale, id)` handler (if configured, and if it returns a string)
6. Returns the raw message ID as-is

### MessageDescriptor with t()

When `t()` receives a `MessageDescriptor` object instead of a string, it uses `descriptor.message` as inline source text if the ID is not found in the catalog:

```ts
i18n.t({ id: 'greeting', message: 'Hello {name}!' }, { name: 'World' })
// Uses descriptor.message if 'greeting' is not in the catalog
```

## parse(message)

Parse an ICU MessageFormat string into an AST (`ASTNode[]`). Hand-written recursive descent parser.

```ts
import { parse } from '@fluenti/core'

// Simple variable
parse('Hello {name}!')
// [{ type: 'text', value: 'Hello ' }, { type: 'variable', name: 'name' }, { type: 'text', value: '!' }]

// Plural
parse('{count, plural, one {# item} other {# items}}')
// [{ type: 'plural', variable: 'count', options: { one: [...], other: [...] } }]

// Select
parse('{gender, select, male {He} female {She} other {They}}')
// [{ type: 'select', variable: 'gender', options: { male: [...], female: [...], other: [...] } }]

// Function with style
parse('{amount, number, currency}')
// [{ type: 'function', variable: 'amount', fn: 'number', style: 'currency' }]
```

### Supported ICU Features

- Simple text
- Variable interpolation: `{name}`
- Plural: `{count, plural, =0 {none} one {# item} other {# items}}`
- Select: `{gender, select, male {He} female {She} other {They}}`
- Selectordinal: `{pos, selectordinal, one {#st} two {#nd} few {#rd} other {#th}}`
- Plural offset: `{count, plural, offset:1 =0 {nobody} one {# other} other {# others}}`
- Functions: `{amount, number}`, `{amount, number, currency}`, `{amount, number, percent}`, `{d, date}`, `{d, date, short}`, `{d, date, long}`, `{d, date, full}`, `{t, time}`, `{t, time, short}`, `{t, time, long}`
- Nested messages within plural/select branches
- `#` substitution inside plural branches (replaced with count minus offset)
- ICU escaping: `''` for literal `'`, `'{'` for literal `{`, `'}'` for literal `}`, `'quoted sequences'`

### AST Node Types

```ts
type ASTNode = TextNode | VariableNode | PluralNode | SelectNode | FunctionNode

interface TextNode {
  type: 'text'
  value: string
}

interface VariableNode {
  type: 'variable'
  name: string        // '#' for plural count substitution
}

interface PluralNode {
  type: 'plural'
  variable: string
  offset?: number     // Plural offset value
  ordinal?: boolean   // true for selectordinal
  options: Record<string, ASTNode[]>  // Keys: '=0', '=1', 'zero', 'one', 'two', 'few', 'many', 'other'
}

interface SelectNode {
  type: 'select'
  variable: string
  options: Record<string, ASTNode[]>  // Keys: any string, must include 'other'
}

interface FunctionNode {
  type: 'function'
  variable: string
  fn: string          // 'number', 'date', 'time'
  style?: string      // 'currency', 'percent', 'short', 'long', 'full'
}
```

### Error Handling

Throws `FluentParseError` on invalid input. The error includes the offset position and a source excerpt with a pointer for diagnostics.

```ts
import { FluentParseError } from '@fluenti/core'

try {
  parse('{count, plural, one {item}}')  // Missing required 'other' option
} catch (e) {
  if (e instanceof FluentParseError) {
    console.log(e.offset)   // Position of the error in the source
    console.log(e.source)   // Original source string
    console.log(e.message)  // Formatted message with excerpt and pointer
  }
}
```

Validation rules:
- Plural and selectordinal must have an `other` option
- Select must have an `other` option
- Identifiers must match `[a-zA-Z0-9_]+`
- All opened `{` must be closed with `}`

## compile(ast, locale?)

Compile a parsed AST into a `CompiledMessage`.

```ts
import { parse, compile } from '@fluenti/core'

// Static text: returns a plain string (zero runtime overhead)
const staticMsg = compile(parse('Hello world'))
// "Hello world"

// Dynamic message: returns a function
const dynamicMsg = compile(parse('Hello {name}!'))
dynamicMsg({ name: 'Alice' })  // "Hello Alice!"

// Plural with locale
const pluralMsg = compile(parse('{count, plural, one {# item} other {# items}}'), 'en')
pluralMsg({ count: 1 })  // "1 item"
pluralMsg({ count: 5 })  // "5 items"

// Select
const selectMsg = compile(parse('{gender, select, male {He} female {She} other {They}}'))
selectMsg({ gender: 'female' })  // "She"

// Function formatting
const numMsg = compile(parse('{amount, number, currency}'), 'en')
numMsg({ amount: 1234.5 })  // "$1,234.50"
```

**Return type**: `CompiledMessage = string | ((values?: Record<string, unknown>) => string)`

**Optimizations**:
- Single text node: returns the string directly
- All text nodes: concatenates and returns a string
- Otherwise: returns a function

**Runtime behavior**:
- Missing values render as `{variableName}` (the placeholder text)
- `#` inside plural branches is replaced with the numeric value minus offset
- Function nodes use `Intl.NumberFormat` and `Intl.DateTimeFormat`
- Exact matches (`=0`, `=1`) in plurals are checked against raw count; CLDR categories are checked against offset-adjusted count
- Default locale is `'en'` if not specified

## interpolate(message, values?, locale?)

One-step convenience function: parse, compile, and execute an ICU message. Results are stored in an LRU cache (500 entries max) keyed by `locale:message`.

```ts
import { interpolate } from '@fluenti/core'

interpolate('Hello {name}!', { name: 'World' })
// "Hello World!"

interpolate('{count, plural, one {# item} other {# items}}', { count: 3 }, 'en')
// "3 items"

// Subsequent calls with the same message+locale hit the cache
interpolate('Hello {name}!', { name: 'Alice' })
// "Hello Alice!" (no re-parse)
```

Parameters:
- `message: string` -- ICU MessageFormat string
- `values?: Record<string, unknown>` -- Interpolation values
- `locale?: Locale` -- BCP 47 locale string (defaults to `'en'`)

## resolvePlural(count, options, locale, ordinal?)

Resolve the correct plural category for a count value. Checks exact matches (`=0`, `=1`, etc.) first, then falls back to CLDR plural categories via `Intl.PluralRules`.

```ts
import { resolvePlural } from '@fluenti/core'

resolvePlural(0, { '=0': 'none', one: 'item', other: 'items' }, 'en')
// '=0' (exact match takes priority)

resolvePlural(1, { one: 'item', other: 'items' }, 'en')
// 'one' (CLDR category)

resolvePlural(5, { one: 'item', other: 'items' }, 'en')
// 'other'

// Ordinal plurals
resolvePlural(1, { one: 'st', two: 'nd', few: 'rd', other: 'th' }, 'en', true)
// 'one'
```

### resolvePluralCategory(count, options, locale, ordinal?)

Same as `resolvePlural` but skips exact match checking. Used internally when exact matches need the raw count but CLDR categories need the offset-adjusted count.

Both functions use cached `Intl.PluralRules` instances per locale+type combination.

## formatDate(value, locale, style?, styles?)

Format dates using named styles backed by `Intl.DateTimeFormat`. Formatter instances are cached.

```ts
import { formatDate } from '@fluenti/core'

formatDate(new Date(), 'en')                       // "Jan 15, 2025" (default style)
formatDate(new Date(), 'en', 'short')               // "1/15/2025"
formatDate(new Date(), 'en', 'long')                // "Wednesday, January 15, 2025"
formatDate(new Date(), 'en', 'time')                // "2:30 PM"
formatDate(new Date(), 'en', 'datetime')            // "Jan 15, 2025, 2:30 PM"
formatDate(Date.now() - 86400000, 'en', 'relative') // "yesterday"
```

### Built-in Date Styles (DEFAULT_DATE_FORMATS)

| Style | Options |
|---|---|
| `default` | `{ year: 'numeric', month: 'short', day: 'numeric' }` |
| `short` | `{ year: 'numeric', month: 'numeric', day: 'numeric' }` |
| `long` | `{ year: 'numeric', month: 'long', day: 'numeric', weekday: 'long' }` |
| `time` | `{ hour: 'numeric', minute: 'numeric' }` |
| `datetime` | `{ year: 'numeric', month: 'short', day: 'numeric', hour: 'numeric', minute: 'numeric' }` |
| `relative` | Delegates to `formatRelativeTime()` |

Custom styles are passed as the 4th argument or configured via `dateFormats` in `createFluent()`. Custom styles are merged over defaults, so you can override built-in styles or add new ones.

A style value can be `Intl.DateTimeFormatOptions` or the string `'relative'` to delegate to relative time formatting.

## formatNumber(value, locale, style?, styles?)

Format numbers using named styles backed by `Intl.NumberFormat`. Formatter instances are cached.

```ts
import { formatNumber } from '@fluenti/core'

formatNumber(1234.56, 'en')                         // "1,234.56"
formatNumber(1234.56, 'de')                         // "1.234,56"
formatNumber(1234.56, 'en', 'currency')             // "$1,234.56" (currency auto-detected from locale)
formatNumber(1234.56, 'ja', 'currency')             // JPY auto-detected
formatNumber(0.856, 'en', 'percent')                // "86%"
formatNumber(1234.5, 'en', 'decimal')               // "1,234.50" (2 decimal places)
```

### Built-in Number Styles (DEFAULT_NUMBER_FORMATS)

| Style | Options |
|---|---|
| `default` | `{}` (locale default formatting) |
| `currency` | `{ style: 'currency', currency: <auto-detected> }` |
| `percent` | `{ style: 'percent' }` |
| `decimal` | `{ minimumFractionDigits: 2, maximumFractionDigits: 2 }` |

The `currency` style auto-detects the currency code from the locale via `LOCALE_CURRENCY_MAP`. Falls back to `USD` for unmapped locales.

### LOCALE_CURRENCY_MAP

Maps locales to default currency codes:

`en` / `en-US` -> `USD`, `en-GB` -> `GBP`, `en-AU` -> `AUD`, `en-CA` -> `CAD`, `zh-CN` -> `CNY`, `zh-TW` -> `TWD`, `zh-HK` -> `HKD`, `ja` / `ja-JP` -> `JPY`, `ko` / `ko-KR` -> `KRW`, `de` / `fr` / `es` / `it` -> `EUR`, `pt-BR` -> `BRL`, `ru` -> `RUB`, `ar` -> `SAR`, `hi` -> `INR`.

### Custom Number Styles

Custom styles can be static `Intl.NumberFormatOptions` or a function `(locale: Locale) => Intl.NumberFormatOptions` for locale-dependent options:

```ts
const i18n = createFluent({
  locale: 'en',
  messages: { en: {} },
  numberFormats: {
    compact: { notation: 'compact' },
    localCurrency: (locale) => ({
      style: 'currency',
      currency: myCurrencyMap[locale] ?? 'USD',
    }),
  },
})
```

## formatRelativeTime(value, locale)

Format a date as a relative time string. Auto-selects the best unit (year, month, week, day, hour, minute, second) based on the time difference from `Date.now()`.

```ts
import { formatRelativeTime } from '@fluenti/core'

formatRelativeTime(Date.now() - 2 * 86400000, 'en')   // "2 days ago"
formatRelativeTime(Date.now() + 3 * 3600000, 'en')     // "in 3 hours"
formatRelativeTime(Date.now() - 60000, 'ja')            // "1 分前"
formatRelativeTime(Date.now() - 500, 'en')              // "now" (less than 1 second)
```

Uses `Intl.RelativeTimeFormat` with `{ numeric: 'auto' }` for natural phrasing (e.g. "yesterday" instead of "1 day ago").

Returns `''` for non-finite timestamps.

## SSR Utilities

### detectLocale(options)

Detect the best locale from a server-side request context. Checks sources in priority order.

```ts
import { detectLocale } from '@fluenti/core'

const locale = detectLocale({
  cookie: getCookie('locale'),              // Priority 1
  query: url.searchParams.get('locale'),    // Priority 2
  path: url.pathname.split('/')[1],         // Priority 3
  headers: request.headers,                 // Priority 4 (Accept-Language)
  available: ['en', 'ja', 'zh-CN'],
  fallback: 'en',                           // Priority 5
})
```

**DetectLocaleOptions**:

| Property | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `cookie` | `string` | No | Locale value extracted from cookie |
| `query` | `string` | No | Locale value from query parameter |
| `path` | `string` | No | Locale value from URL path segment |
| `headers` | `Headers \| Record<string, string>` | No | Request headers (reads Accept-Language) |
| `available` | `Locale[]` | Yes | List of supported locales |
| `fallback` | `Locale` | Yes | Fallback locale if no source matches |

Accept-Language header parsing:
- Supports quality values: `en-US,en;q=0.9,zh-CN;q=0.8`
- Sorted by quality (highest first)
- Wildcard `*` is ignored
- Each candidate is matched via `negotiateLocale()`

Locale matching for cookie/query/path is case-insensitive and supports partial matches (e.g. `en` matches `en-US`).

### getSSRLocaleScript(locale)

Generate an XSS-safe `<script>` tag for injecting the detected locale into SSR HTML.

```ts
import { getSSRLocaleScript } from '@fluenti/core'

getSSRLocaleScript('zh-CN')
// '<script>window.__FLUENTI_LOCALE__="zh-CN"</script>'
```

Escapes special characters (`\`, `"`, `<`, `>`, `&`, `'`) to prevent XSS injection.

### getHydratedLocale(fallback?)

Read the SSR-injected locale on the client side. Returns `window.__FLUENTI_LOCALE__` if it exists and is a string, otherwise returns the fallback (defaults to `'en'`).

```ts
import { getHydratedLocale } from '@fluenti/core'

// On the client, after SSR injected the script tag:
const locale = getHydratedLocale('en')
// Returns the SSR-detected locale, or 'en' if not available
```

Safe to call on the server (returns fallback when `window` is undefined).

## Locale Utilities

### negotiateLocale(requested, available, fallback?)

BCP 47 locale negotiation. Accepts a single locale or ordered list of preferred locales.

```ts
import { negotiateLocale } from '@fluenti/core'

negotiateLocale('en-US', ['en', 'ja', 'zh-CN'])         // 'en' (language match)
negotiateLocale('zh-Hans-CN', ['en', 'zh-CN'])           // 'zh-CN' (region match)
negotiateLocale(['fr', 'en'], ['en', 'ja'])              // 'en' (second preference matches)
negotiateLocale('ko', ['en', 'ja'], 'en')                // 'en' (no match, fallback)
negotiateLocale('pt-BR', ['pt', 'en'])                   // 'pt' (language match)
```

Matching strategy (in order):
1. Exact match (case-insensitive, normalized)
2. Language + region match (ignoring script)
3. Language-only match
4. Fallback or first available locale

### parseLocale(locale)

Parse a BCP 47 locale string into its components.

```ts
import { parseLocale } from '@fluenti/core'
import type { ParsedLocale } from '@fluenti/core'

parseLocale('en')            // { language: 'en' }
parseLocale('en-US')         // { language: 'en', region: 'US' }
parseLocale('zh-Hans-CN')    // { language: 'zh', script: 'Hans', region: 'CN' }
```

**ParsedLocale**: `{ language: string; script?: string; region?: string }`

### isRTL(locale)

Check if a locale uses a right-to-left script. Returns `boolean`.

```ts
import { isRTL } from '@fluenti/core'

isRTL('ar')      // true
isRTL('ar-SA')   // true
isRTL('he')      // true
isRTL('fa')      // true (Persian)
isRTL('ur')      // true (Urdu)
isRTL('en')      // false
isRTL('ja')      // false
```

Supported RTL languages: Arabic (`ar`), Hebrew (`he`), Persian (`fa`), Urdu (`ur`), Pashto (`ps`), Sindhi (`sd`), Uyghur (`ug`), Central Kurdish/Sorani (`ckb`), Dhivehi (`dv`), Yiddish (`yi`), N'Ko (`nqo`).

### getDirection(locale)

Returns `'rtl'` or `'ltr'` for a locale. Useful for setting the `dir` attribute:

```tsx
<html lang={locale} dir={getDirection(locale)}>
```

## msg Tagged Template

Create lazy message descriptors with auto-generated IDs. Uses FNV-1a hashing to generate stable, short IDs from the message content. Expressions become positional ICU placeholders.

```ts
import { msg } from '@fluenti/core'

// Tagged template: expressions become {0}, {1}, etc.
const greeting = msg`Hello ${name}`
// { id: '<fnv1a-hash>', message: 'Hello {0}' }

// Use with t()
i18n.t(greeting)
```

### msg.descriptor(desc)

Pass through an explicit message descriptor unchanged. Useful for static extraction:

```ts
const desc = msg.descriptor({ id: 'greeting', message: 'Hello {name}', comment: 'Main greeting' })
// { id: 'greeting', message: 'Hello {name}', comment: 'Main greeting' }
```

## Catalog Class

In-memory message catalog manager. Supports namespace-aware message IDs (e.g. `'common:greeting'`).

```ts
import { Catalog } from '@fluenti/core'

const catalog = new Catalog()

// Load messages for a locale
catalog.set('en', { greeting: 'Hello!', 'common:save': 'Save' })

// Lookup
catalog.get('en', 'greeting')        // 'Hello!'
catalog.get('en', 'common:save')     // 'Save'
catalog.get('en', 'missing')         // undefined
catalog.get('fr', 'greeting')        // undefined

// Check existence
catalog.has('en', 'greeting')        // true
catalog.has('fr', 'greeting')        // false

// List locales
catalog.getLocales()                 // ['en']

// Merge: subsequent set() calls merge with existing messages
catalog.set('en', { farewell: 'Goodbye!' })
catalog.get('en', 'greeting')        // 'Hello!' (preserved)
catalog.get('en', 'farewell')        // 'Goodbye!' (added)
```

Messages can be `string` (static) or `(values?: Record<string, unknown>) => string` (dynamic, compiled from ICU).

## All Exported Types

```ts
// --- Core Types ---

type Locale = string
type CompiledMessage = string | ((values?: Record<string, unknown>) => string)
type Messages = Record<string, CompiledMessage>
type AllMessages = Record<Locale, Messages>

// --- Configuration ---

interface FluentConfig {
  locale: Locale
  fallbackLocale?: Locale
  messages: AllMessages
  missing?: (locale: Locale, id: string) => string | undefined
}

interface FluentConfigExtended extends FluentConfig {
  namespaceMapping?: NamespaceMapping
  dateFormats?: DateFormatOptions
  numberFormats?: NumberFormatOptions
  fallbackChain?: Record<string, Locale[]>
  externalCatalogs?: Array<{ package: string; catalogDir: string }>
}

// --- Instance ---

interface FluentInstance {
  locale: Locale
  t(id: string | MessageDescriptor, values?: Record<string, unknown>): string
  setLocale(locale: Locale): void
  loadMessages(locale: Locale, messages: Messages): void
  getLocales(): Locale[]
}

interface FluentInstanceExtended extends FluentInstance {
  d: (value: Date | number, style?: string) => string
  n: (value: number, style?: string) => string
  format(message: string, values?: Record<string, unknown>): string
  /** @deprecated Use format() instead */
  tRaw(message: string, values?: Record<string, unknown>): string
}

// --- Messages ---

interface MessageDescriptor {
  id: string
  message?: string
  comment?: string
  context?: string
}

interface ExtractedMessage {
  id: string
  message?: string
  comment?: string
  context?: string
  origin: { file: string; line: number; column?: number }
}

// --- AST ---

type ASTNode = TextNode | VariableNode | PluralNode | SelectNode | FunctionNode

interface TextNode { type: 'text'; value: string }
interface VariableNode { type: 'variable'; name: string }
interface PluralNode {
  type: 'plural'
  variable: string
  offset?: number
  ordinal?: boolean
  options: Record<string, ASTNode[]>
}
interface SelectNode {
  type: 'select'
  variable: string
  options: Record<string, ASTNode[]>
}
interface FunctionNode {
  type: 'function'
  variable: string
  fn: string
  style?: string
}

// --- SSR ---

interface DetectLocaleOptions {
  cookie?: string
  query?: string
  path?: string
  headers?: Headers | Record<string, string>
  available: Locale[]
  fallback: Locale
}

type DetectLocale = (options: DetectLocaleOptions) => Locale
type GetSSRLocaleScript = (locale: Locale) => string
type GetHydratedLocale = (fallback?: Locale) => Locale

// --- Formatting ---

interface DateFormatOptions {
  [styleName: string]: Intl.DateTimeFormatOptions | 'relative'
}

interface NumberFormatOptions {
  [styleName: string]: Intl.NumberFormatOptions | ((locale: Locale) => Intl.NumberFormatOptions)
}

type FormatDateFn = (value: Date | number, style?: string) => string
type FormatNumberFn = (value: number, style?: string) => string

// --- Locale ---

interface ParsedLocale {
  language: string
  script?: string
  region?: string
}

interface NamespaceMapping {
  [globPattern: string]: string
}

// --- Lazy Messages ---

type MsgTaggedTemplate = (strings: TemplateStringsArray, ...exprs: unknown[]) => MessageDescriptor
type MsgDescriptor = (descriptor: MessageDescriptor) => MessageDescriptor

// --- CLI/Build Config ---

interface FluentiConfig {
  sourceLocale: Locale
  locales: Locale[]
  catalogDir: string
  format: 'json' | 'po'
  include: string[]
  exclude?: string[]
  compileOutDir: string
  devWarnings?: boolean
  strictBuild?: boolean
  namespaceMapping?: Record<string, string>
  fallbackChain?: Record<string, Locale[]>
  externalCatalogs?: Array<{ package: string; catalogDir: string }>
  strictThreshold?: number
  splitting?: 'dynamic' | 'static' | false
  defaultBuildLocale?: Locale
}
```

## Documentation

- Full docs: [fluenti.dev](https://fluenti.dev)
- Repository: [GitHub](https://github.com/usefluenti/fluenti)
